description:
You are a QA engineer who needs to confirm that the test environment can
    correctly interpret the United-Kingdom time zone.  
    1. Produce a log file named “uk-time.log” in your home directory
       (full path: /home/user/uk-time.log).  
    2. The file must contain **exactly one line** (plus the mandatory trailing
       newline) showing the current date-and-time for the Europe/London time
       zone.  
    3. The line must follow this precise format (the automated test will perform
       a strict pattern match):

           YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS ZZZ⏎

       • YYYY-MM-DD  → 4-digit year, 2-digit month, 2-digit day  
       • HH:MM:SS    → 24-hour time  
       • ZZZ         → the time-zone abbreviation, which for Europe/London must
                       be either “BST” (British Summer Time) or “GMT” (Greenwich
                       Mean Time) depending on the current date  
       • The very last character in the file must be a newline (⏎).  
       • No leading/trailing spaces anywhere on the line.  
    4. Use **a single terminal command** to generate the content and write it to
       /home/user/uk-time.log.  (If you need a redirection operator, include it
       in that same command.)  

    The automated check will:
       • Verify that /home/user/uk-time.log exists.  
       • Ensure the file has exactly one non-empty line plus the final newline.  
       • Confirm that the line matches the required pattern and that the zone
         abbreviation is either BST or GMT.  
    Nothing else needs to be created or modified.
truth:
<!--
    Verification data for the automated grader.
    The grader will read /home/user/uk-time.log and assert that its sole line
    matches this regular expression (POSIX Extended):

        ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} (BST|GMT)\n?$

    • Path that must exist: /home/user/uk-time.log
    • Exactly one line followed by at most one newline.
    -->
name: task_000000_07901e1d
